deep_dive.title: On-board Remote-Sensing Foundation Models for Unsupervised Change Detection of Disaster Events
deep_dive.abstract: Remote Sensing Foundation Models (RSFMs) have emerged as a powerful alternative to supervised models for Earth Observation, allowing satellites to autonomously trigger high-resolution captures or adjust tasking parameters upon detecting an anomaly, thereby maximizing the utility of the mission's limited power and computational resources. RSFMs are versatile, unified encoders that optimize onboard storage for multiple orbital applications while ensuring high-fidelity feature extraction. In particular, unsupervised change detection with RSFMs offers a well-informed and transformative path for disaster monitoring without expensive labels. In this paper, we present a novel unsupervised detection method based on ResNet (RSFM) + FPN which identifies a wide spectrum of anomalies by detecting subtle semantic shifts in the latent space between successive orbital passes. By relying on an untrained FPN architecture and its intrinsic priors, the system achieves efficient image-level generation and higher resolution mapping with minimal effort (training-free) compared to previous proposals (patch-based, trained). And by replacing tailored models with RSFMs, we can achieve comparable results through an approach that eliminates the need for bespoke training and extensive development effort and adds customization, while ensuring high-performance generalization across diverse terrains and sensors.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: remote sensing foundation model for change detection
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 遥感基础模型用于变化检测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 遥感基础模型用于变化检测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 遥感基础模型用于变化检测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper presents an unsupervised change detection method using Remote Sensing Foundation Models (RSFMs) based on ResNet+FPN, enabling onboard autonomous disaster monitoring without labels.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出一种基于遥感基础模型（RSFMs）的无监督变化检测方法，采用ResNet+FPN架构，实现星上自主灾害监测，无需标注数据，在卫星资源受限条件下高效检测异常并触发高分辨率捕获。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: What are foundation models for remote sensing?

deep_dive.title: ABACUS: Adapting Unified Foundation Model for Bridging Image Count Understanding and Generation
deep_dive.abstract: ABACUS is a unified vision-language model that handles object counting, crowd counting, referring-expression counting, and count-faithful image generation without any benchmark-specific training required. Our model is built on existing 3B-parameter unified foundation model and is adapted for object localization tasks using three key innovations: density-aware adaptive zooming with objectness maps for spatial grounding; a boundary-aware count policy via GRPO to eliminate crop-boundary errors; and a cycle-consistent GRPO strategy where the understanding branch self-critiques generated outputs, closing the understanding-generation gap without any external annotations. ABACUS achieves state-of-the-art results across seven benchmarks, outperforming both task-specific specialists and larger generalist models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: adapts unified foundation model for multimodal image count understanding and generation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 适配统一基础模型进行多模态图像计数理解与生成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 适配统一基础模型进行多模态图像计数理解与生成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 适配统一基础模型进行多模态图像计数理解与生成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ABACUS adapts a 3B-parameter unified foundation model to handle object counting, crowd counting, and count-faithful image generation using density-aware adaptive zooming and cycle-consistent GRPO, achieving SOTA on seven benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出ABACUS，在现有3B参数统一基础模型上通过三项创新适配实现图像计数理解与生成：密度感知自适应缩放与目标图用于空间定位，边界感知计数策略消除裁剪边界误差，循环一致性GRPO使理解分支自我批判生成输出。无需基准特定训练即在七个基准上达到最先进结果。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出ABACUS，在现有3B参数统一基础模型上通过三项创新适配实现图像计数理解与生成：密度感知自适应缩放与目标图用于空间定位，边界感知计数策略消除裁剪边界误差，循环一致性GRPO使理解分支自我批判生成输出。无需基准特定训练即在七个基准上达到最先进结果。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请帮我查找有关世界模型、多模态基础模型、时空模型相关的论文, especially work that connects or combines: world model for planning and control; multimodal foundation model learning; spatiotemporal modeling in deep learning; transformer for spatiotemporal data; neural radiance field for 3D scene representation; multi modal foundation models for understanding and generation; world models for reinforcement learning; spatiotemporal representation learning; integrating world models with foundation models.

quick_skim.title: M^2C-EvDet: Multi-Domain Multi-Order Cross-Modal Knowledge Distillation for Event-based Object Detection
quick_skim.abstract: Event-based object Detection (EvDet), as a biologically inspired visual perception paradigm, demonstrates superior performance in scenarios demanding high temporal resolution and a wide dynamic range. Nevertheless, the inherent sparse representations and inadequate visual semantics of event data result in a considerable performance disparity between EvDet and frame-based object detection. Previous works attempt to alleviate this cross-modal discrepancy through knowledge distillation, yet they only focus on spatial visual semantics or pair-wise relational information, thus limiting performance in more complex scenarios. To address this challenge, this paper proposes M^2C-EvDet, a Multi-domain and Multi-order Cross-modal knowledge distillation framework for EvDet. Built upon frequency learning and hypergraph computation, M^2C-EvDet integrates two specialized modules: Adaptive Frequency-Decoupled Feature Distillation (AF^2D^2) and Multi-Order Relational Distillation (MORD).
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-domain cross-modal knowledge distillation for event-based detection
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多域跨模态知识蒸馏用于事件检测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多域跨模态知识蒸馏用于事件检测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多域跨模态知识蒸馏用于事件检测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes M^2C-EvDet for multi-domain multi-order cross-modal knowledge distillation to improve event-based object detection by leveraging spatial and relational information across domains.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该工作针对事件相机与帧相机之间的跨模态性能差距，提出一种多域多阶跨模态知识蒸馏方法M^2C-EvDet。方法不仅利用空间视觉语义，还引入高阶关系信息，在多个域上蒸馏知识。实验表明该方法在复杂场景下有效提升了事件检测的性能。该工作为多源域适应方法提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该工作针对事件相机与帧相机之间的跨模态性能差距，提出一种多域多阶跨模态知识蒸馏方法M^2C-EvDet。方法不仅利用空间视觉语义，还引入高阶关系信息，在多个域上蒸馏知识。实验表明该方法在复杂场景下有效提升了事件检测的性能。该工作为多源域适应方法提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: RaysUp: Ultra-light Universal Feature Upsampling via Geometry-Aware Ray Representation
quick_skim.abstract: Pre-trained Vision Foundation Models (VFMs) have become central to modern computer vision due to their powerful semantic representations and strong generalization ability. However, their patchified or pooled outputs are inherently low-resolution, limiting their effectiveness in tasks requiring fine-grained, pixel-level reasoning. Existing feature upsampling approaches either degrade semantic fidelity or rely on VFM-specific retraining and heavy architectures, hindering efficiency and scalability. To address these challenges, we propose RaysUp, an ultra-lightweight, task-agnostic, and VFM-agnostic feature upsampling framework that reconstructs high-resolution feature maps at arbitrary resolutions. Unlike conventional 2D interpolation or attention-based schemes, RaysUp lifts feature reconstruction into a geometry-aware ray domain. Specifically, we introduce a Spatially Decoupled Guidance Encoder for direction-aware guidance encoding, an Any-Resolution Cross-Attention mechanism for resolution-flexible reconstruction, and a novel Ray Positional Encoding (RayPE) that injects implicit 3D geometric priors via 6D Plucker ray coordinates. Finally, a Geometry-Aware Neighborhood Attention module further ensures content-adaptive bilateral aggregation while preserving geometric consistency. Extensive experiments across diverse dense prediction tasks demonstrate that RaysUp achieves state-of-the-art performance while using only 16% of the parameters of AnyUp and delivering approximately 7x faster inference. These results highlight a substantially improved accuracy-efficiency trade-off and establish RaysUp as a practical and scalable solution for universal feature upsampling. Code is available at https://github.com/MAP-RaysUp/RaysUp.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: feature upsampling for vision foundation models, applicable to remote sensing
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对视觉基础模型的特征上采样方法，可应用于遥感
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对视觉基础模型的特征上采样方法，可应用于遥感
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对视觉基础模型的特征上采样方法，可应用于遥感
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: RaysUp is an ultra-light feature upsampling framework for pre-trained vision foundation models, reconstructing high-resolution features.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 预训练视觉基础模型输出分辨率低，现有上采样方法损失语义或需特定重训练。本文提出RaysUp，轻量级、任务无关的上采样框架，利用几何感知射线表示重建任意分辨率的高分辨率特征图，保持语义保真度。

quick_skim.title: What's in an Earth Embedding? An Explainability Analysis of Location Encoders
quick_skim.abstract: Geographic implicit neural representations (INRs) learn to map any coordinate on Earth to a location embedding, implicitly encoding geospatial data into the weights of a neural network. Location embeddings are widely used off the shelf as general-purpose geospatial representations, yet users lack principled tools to audit what geographic or semantic information these embeddings capture. In this work, we analyze the information content of geographic INRs through their location embeddings. We decompose these embeddings into human-interpretable features$\unicode{x2014}$namely, (i) sparse latent concepts, (ii) natural language concepts, and (iii) visual features. The latent concept embeddings are learned using sparse autoencoders. To recover natural language concepts, we apply sparse linear concept embeddings (SpLiCE) over a predefined geospatial dictionary. Finally, visual features are extracted using saliency maps derived from CLIP Surgery. We show that location embeddings can be decomposed into human-interpretable representations while retaining high reconstruction capability, revealing interpretable geographic structures such as forests, deserts, and urban features. Across methods, sparse decompositions expose systematic differences in encoded information, ranging from urban structures to broader biome and climate signals, and pretraining-space saliency maps further highlight complementary features such as roads and landmarks. We hope this work provides a first step toward interpretable geospatial representations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Analyzes Earth embeddings from geographic INRs, relevant to earth observation foundation models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 分析地球位置编码，与地球观测基础模型相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 分析地球位置编码，与地球观测基础模型相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 分析地球位置编码，与地球观测基础模型相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Explains information content of location embeddings from geographic implicit neural representations, decomposing into interpretable features for geospatial foundation model auditing.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文分析了地理隐式神经表示的位置编码信息内容，通过稀疏自编码器将嵌入分解为稀疏潜在概念、自然语言概念和视觉特征等可解释特征，为解决地理空间基础模型缺乏可解释性工具的问题提供了审计方法。实验表明分解后的特征能有效反映地理和语义信息。该工作提升了地球观测基础模型的透明度和可信度。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文分析了地理隐式神经表示的位置编码信息内容，通过稀疏自编码器将嵌入分解为稀疏潜在概念、自然语言概念和视觉特征等可解释特征，为解决地理空间基础模型缺乏可解释性工具的问题提供了审计方法。实验表明分解后的特征能有效反映地理和语义信息。该工作提升了地球观测基础模型的透明度和可信度。

quick_skim.title: Methane-Plume Segmentation From Hyperspectral Satellite Imagery Via Multimodal Deep Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Efficient detection of methane plumes is crucial for understanding and mitigating global warming, as accurately identifying and segmenting them in earth observation imagery remain essential for large-scale monitoring. In this work, we propose a multimodal deep learning model that integrates a feature-guided methane enhancement (FGME) mechanism which injects physically meaningful methane cues into transformer-based RGB representations at multiple semantic scales. Our method is evaluated on the MPDataset, where it outperforms the state-of-the-art with improvements of +0.92 in MIoU, +0.87 in MPrecision and +1.01 in Recall. Notably, these gains are obtained with a substantially lower computational cost than other high-performing architectures, resulting in a favorable accuracy-efficiency trade-off for large-scale methane monitoring. These results highlight the potential of efficient multimodal fusion strategies for accurate and scalable methane plume segmentation in real-world remote sensing applications.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: transformer-based multimodal model for remote sensing methane plume segmentation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于Transformer的多模态遥感甲烷羽流分割模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于Transformer的多模态遥感甲烷羽流分割模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于Transformer的多模态遥感甲烷羽流分割模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: A multimodal deep learning model with feature-guided methane enhancement integrates transformer-based RGB representations for methane plume segmentation from hyperspectral satellite imagery, outperforming SOTA.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对高光谱卫星影像中甲烷羽流分割任务，该文提出多模态深度学习模型，通过特征引导甲烷增强机制将物理信息注入Transformer的RGB表示中。在MPDataset上取得最优性能，MIoU提升0.92，同时计算成本更低。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对高光谱卫星影像中甲烷羽流分割任务，该文提出多模态深度学习模型，通过特征引导甲烷增强机制将物理信息注入Transformer的RGB表示中。在MPDataset上取得最优性能，MIoU提升0.92，同时计算成本更低。
